title: genista hirsuta subsp. algarbiensis
intro: Genista hirsuta subsp. algarbiensis, also known as the Algarve broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to southern Europe. This shrub has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its leaves and stems containing compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Genista hirsuta subsp. algarbiensis include its potential use in treating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
